sound_type: aerial acrobatics + call burst -- pod performing leaps and somersaults combined with whistles, burst-pulse calls, and echolocation; impact splash of multiple dolphins re-entering water; social vocal burst during active aerial display; E=7
ecosystem: Open channel -- Puget Sound, Strait of Juan de Fuca, Salish Sea; resident year-round with prey-dependent distribution variability
conservation_status: Least Concern (IUCN). North Pacific population ~900,000-1,000,000. Historical drift net bycatch reduced after UN moratorium 1993. Threats: prey availability, vessel noise, PCB/DDT/mercury contamination, nearshore entanglement.

coordinates.description: Resident in Puget Sound and Salish Sea; distribution varies with herring and anchovy prey availability. Common in Strait of Juan de Fuca and deep channels.

acoustic_data.primary_event: Aerial acrobatics + call burst: impact splash of leaping dolphins (130-200 lb animals re-entering water at speed) combined with simultaneous pod vocalizations (whistles, burst-pulse calls). Multiple individuals simultaneously airborne in a 10-50 animal pod produces significant combined acoustic event.
acoustic_data.whistles: Frequency-modulated tonal signals, primary social communication. Stereotyped and variable whistle types for group cohesion and coordination. Unlike bottlenose dolphin (individual signature whistles), Pacific White-sided Dolphin uses general whistle types.
acoustic_data.burst_pulse: Rapid pulse trains with rough acoustic quality; associated with social excitement and emotional arousal. Call rate increases during aerial activity and group excitement. Social context signal.
acoustic_data.echolocation: Broadband clicks, 20-100 kHz, primarily ultrasonic. Used for navigation and prey detection. Active during cooperative foraging on schooling fish.
acoustic_data.e7_calibration: Group-generated exuberance at E=7. Less than orca breach (E=9) or Steller Sea Lion bull roar (E=8). More than Dall's Porpoise rooster tail (E=6). The energy is collective: multiple animals, multiple acoustic events simultaneously.

nodes.title: Delphinidae Returns at Degree 282
nodes.description: After orca arc (270-276, all Orcinus orca) and Phocoenidae (281, Dall's Porpoise), Delphinidae returns with a different representative. Pacific White-sided Dolphin: smaller, more acrobatic, less stable social structure than orca pods. Family's range documented across multiple entries.

nodes.title: Aerial Acrobatics -- Full Breach and Somersault
nodes.description: Full breach (entire body clears surface) and somersault (long-axis rotation while airborne). Functions: communication, parasite removal, play, social bonding, prey herding. Multiple simultaneous aerial behaviors in a pod of 10-50 produce a significant combined splash and acoustic event.

nodes.title: Pod Vocal Burst -- Whistles + Burst-Pulse + Echolocation
nodes.description: Three vocalization types: frequency-modulated whistles (social), burst-pulse calls (excitement/arousal, increases during aerial activity), echolocation clicks (20-100 kHz, foraging). All three increase during active social display. The burst-pulse component is the social excitement signal.

nodes.title: Cooperative Foraging -- Bait Ball and Vocal Coordination
nodes.description: Pod surrounds schooling fish into bait ball near surface. Individuals take turns rushing through while others maintain formation. Intense acoustic activity: echolocation, social calls, burst-pulse. Multi-species associations with Steller Sea Lions (degree 283) and humpback whales at productive sites.

cross_references.relationship: Delphinidae established at 270. Pacific White-sided Dolphin returns family at 282. Different representative: smaller, more acrobatic, less socially stable. Family range documented by both entries.
Phocoenidae E=6 → Delphinidae E=7. Speed and individual quiet (281) to group acrobatics and vocal (282). Family transition; energy rises by 1 unit.
Otariidae enters at 283, E=8. Arc ascends: dolphin pod E=7 → Steller Sea Lion E=8. Multi-species association: Pacific White-sided Dolphins and Steller Sea Lions both aggregate at herring schools.
E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Capitol Hill punk pop collective exuberance paired with Pacific White-sided Dolphin pod collective exuberance. Both social, both group-generated, both at exactly E=7.
